title: Stenostomum acreanum
intro: Stenostomum acreanum, also known as "Herbe à Pain" or "Bread Herb", is a tropical plant species belonging to the Acanthaceae family, traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Stenostomum acreanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to promote digestive health, relieve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Stenostomum acreanum is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the order Gentianales and the family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as coffee or bedstraw family. Stenostomum acreanum is further classified into the genus Stenostomum and the species S. acreanum. This classification helps to identify and distinguish the plant from other similar species within the Rubiaceae family.
